title: cat, echo, head, tail & Pagers
content:
### cat — Concatenate and Display

```bash
cat file.txt                # display file
cat file1 file2 > merged    # concatenate files
cat -n file.txt             # number all lines
cat -b file.txt             # number non-blank lines
```

### echo — Print Text

```bash
echo "Hello World"
echo -n "No newline"        # suppress trailing newline
echo -e "tab\there"         # interpret escape sequences
```

### printf — Formatted Print

```bash
printf "Name: %s\nAge: %d\n" "Alice" 30
```

### tac — Reverse cat

```bash
tac file.txt                # display file in reverse line order
```

### nl — Number Lines

```bash
nl file.txt                 # numbered lines (more options than cat -n)
```

### head / tail

```bash
head -20 file.txt           # first 20 lines
head -n -5 file.txt         # all except last 5 lines
tail -20 file.txt           # last 20 lines
tail -f /var/log/syslog     # follow (watch live updates)
tail -n +10 file.txt        # from line 10 to end
```

### less / more — Pagers

```bash
less file.txt               # scrollable viewer (q to quit)
less -N file.txt            # with line numbers
more file.txt               # simpler pager (space for next page)
```

### Common Patterns

```bash
tail -100f access.log       # watch last 100 lines of a log
cat file.txt | head -5      # pipe to head
```
codeExample:
cat -n /etc/passwd | head -10
echo "Home: $HOME"
printf "%-10s %s\n" "User" "Shell"
tail -f /var/log/syslog &
less /etc/os-release

quiz.question: What does `tail -f` do?
options: Follows a file and shows new lines as they're written
Shows only the first few lines
Searches for a pattern in a file

quiz.question: What is the difference between `cat` and `tac`?
options: tac displays lines in reverse order
tac uses tabs instead of spaces
cat can only display one file
